title: Fusion of light nuclei in a multicluster realization of the three-body problem
abstract: This work describes a few-body dynamics method based on the Faddeev integral equations in momentum space for determining the total cross sections of fusion and breakup reactions with two- and three-body final channels in the continuum, employing a cluster representation of the colliding nuclei. Total cross sections were obtained for the reactions $^3\text{He}(T,D)^4\text{He}$, $^3\text{He}(T,np)^4\text{He}$, $^3\text{He}(T,nD)^3\text{He}$, $^3\text{He}(^3\text{He},2p)^4\text{He}$, $^3\text{He}(^3\text{He},pD)^3\text{He}$, $^7\text{Li}(^3\text{He},\phantom{0}^4\text{He})^6\text{Li}$, $^7\text{Li}(^3\text{He},D^4\text{He})^4\text{He}$, and $^7\text{Li}(^3\text{He},T^3\text{He})^4\text{He}$, in which both the projectile and the target nucleus were treated in a cluster representation. The work also implements a two-potential method to determine the Coulomb $t-$matrix and to account for Coulomb effects in short-range dynamics in momentum space. Calculations of the initial-state Coulomb interaction were performed; furthermore, an estimate was obtained for the magnitude of the off-shell effect of the Coulomb $t-$matrix, as well as the magnitude of the atomic electron anti-screening effect on the Coulomb interaction of the colliding nuclei. The calculated contribution of the cluster mechanism to the total cross section of the considered fusion reactions in the kinetic energy range $T\in[1~\text{keV},20~\text{MeV}]$ is in good agreement with known experimental data.
summary: This paper presents a theoretical framework using Faddeev integral equations to calculate fusion and breakup cross sections for light nuclei. By using a multicluster model, the author accounts for the internal structure of the colliding nuclei and the complex long-range Coulomb interactions. The results show strong agreement with existing experimental data for various helium and lithium reactions. The study also investigates the impact of atomic electron anti-screening on these nuclear processes.
main_problem: Determining total cross sections for fusion and breakup reactions of light nuclei while accurately accounting for long-range Coulomb interactions and electron anti-screening effects.
main_result: The multicluster approach provides total cross sections for various helium and lithium fusion reactions that are in good agreement with experimental data, specifically demonstrating the importance of the cluster mechanism and Coulomb effects.
method: The work employs Faddeev integral equations in momentum space using a two-potential method to handle the Coulomb t-matrix and a cluster representation for the colliding nuclei.
model_or_system: A multicluster realization of the three-body problem involving light nuclei such as 3He, T, D, 4He, and 7Li, where nuclei are treated as bound clusters of nucleons.
key_observables: Total cross sections, astrophysical S-factors, breakup amplitudes, and Coulomb t-matrix off-shell effects.
important_parameters: Kinetic energy range T in [1 keV, 20 MeV], Coulomb screening radius, and Sommerfeld parameter.
main_assumptions: The calculation uses a truncated iteration of the Faddeev equations (up to n=2) and assumes specific cluster structures for the projectile and target nuclei.
figures_summary: The notes mention a Figure 4, but a full summary of all figures is not available from the provided text.
paper_structure: The paper progresses from the theoretical necessity of Faddeev equations to the implementation of the multicluster model, followed by the treatment of Coulomb effects and the presentation of calculated cross sections compared to experimental data.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 4.1: Total cross section for Coulomb DT scattering. Rutherford scattering (magenta dash-dotted line) is compared with contributions from the screened Coulomb potential VR and the unscreened Coulomb potential VC (in the limit R →∞). Calculations for the Coulomb TC-matrix (3.0.7) and the Coulomb potential VC(TC) (obtained from the solution of equation (4.1.1)) are also presented. The right-hand scale shows the relative deviations (in percentages) between calculations performed with signiﬁcantly diﬀerent constants C for the screening radius (3.0.5).

Figure 4.2: Left: Relative percentage contributions to the total cross section for elastic DT scattering and the T(D, n)4He reaction with a non-zero short-range interaction VS (2.3.1), accounting for the Coulomb perturbation of the short-range resolvent G0(TC), and with an additional inclusion of the pole contribution (3.0.13) from the initial state interaction. Right: Total cross section for the T(D, n)4He reaction obtained in this work with various Coulomb contributions, including estimates of oﬀ-shell Coulomb eﬀects using formula (3.0.14), compared with evaluated nuclear data from the ENDF library [49].

Figure 4.3: Total cross section for the elastic Coulomb scattering of deuterons by eT and µT atoms in the three-body Born approximation (3b-Born using formula (4.1.2)) and according to formula (2.2.4) (full), including the case where integral terms are neglected (0-term). For com- parison, pure Coulomb DT scattering calculations are presented, and the ionization thresholds for the electron (eT) and muonic (µT) atoms are indicated by short vertical dashes for clarity.

Figure 4.4: Left panel: Total cross sections for the reactions 3He(T, np)4He and 3He(T, D)4He obtained without considering Coulomb repulsion (dashed line for the 3He(T, np)4He process) and with Coulomb repulsion taken into account, where results were obtained both by employing the Gamow factor (lower bound of the shaded area) and by using the square of function (3.0.1) with a non-trivial angular dependence and a screening radius r ≡R from formula (3.0.5). Experimental data are from [50, 51, 52, 53, 54] Right panel: Analogous calculations of the total cross section for the 3He(3He, 2p)4He reaction. Experimental data are from [55, 56, 57, 58, 59, 60, 61].

Figure 4.5: Total cross section for the 7Li(3He, 4He)6Li reaction, calculated using two-channel coupled Lippmann-Schwinger equations (2.3.4) and based on three-body Faddeev dynamics with the target nucleus represented as a 4He −T cluster system in a JP = 3/2−state in the L = 1 partial wave. Experimental data for the ground state of 6Li are from [40].
